PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-05-2026 Continued To Jul-14-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were PES-23-306792 The Estate of Patrick Courtney and PES-26-309124 The Estate of Lola L. Courtney.

Appearances:
Thomas Wrobel for petitioner and with Stephen Courtney (via Zoom).
Ryan Meckfessel for special administrator Debra Dolch, who was also present.
Byron Sun for objector Pu Guang Xie (via Zoom).

Discussion was held regarding mediation, that there is outstanding discovery items that need to be addressed before they can attend mediation, that the current litigation in Ireland regarding real property is still on-going, that a creditors claim should be pursued on the real property in Ireland, and that parties are seeking a special administrator to be assigned to this matter. The Court encourages parties to continue talks and find a mutually agreed special administrator.

The matters are continued to July 14, 2026, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: A. Datt. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Feb-10-2026 Continued To May-05-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Wrobel for petitioner Stephen Courtney (via Zoom).
Ryan Meckfessel for special administrator Debra Dolch, who was also present.
Byron Sun for objector Pu Guang Xie (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Amended Petition for Probate of Will and Letters of Administration with Will Annexed.

The Court inquired regarding the status of the original will reportedly located in Ireland and the absence of any certified documentation or confirmed probate proceedings there. Mr. Wrobel reported that the original will remained in the possession of Irish counsel, that no certified copy had been lodged with any court, and that no probate proceedings in Ireland had been confirmed.

Mr. Meckfessel requested that the special administrator's authority be expanded from limited to full authority due to difficulties obtaining financial information from Bank of America. The Court declined to expand the authority at this time, continued the special administration under limited authority, and directed that any request for expanded authority be made by ex parte application.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to May 5, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow further investigation into the status of any proceedings in Ireland and directed that information be provided regarding any case numbers, filings, counsel, and determinations concerning the will.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-09-2025 Continued To Feb-10-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Thomas Wrobel for petitioner Stephen Courtney (via Zoom).
Ryan Meckfessel for special administrator Debra Dolch, who was also present.

Mr. Meckfessel updated the court on the passing of Mr. Rennie and the passing of Lola Courtney.

The Court waives sanctions on Mr. Rennie.

Discussion was held regarding a global settlement, that the legacy store in Ireland has closed down that was partially owned by Mr. Courtney, that the will is located in Ireland with the store manager Paul but he has been hesitant to release the will to anyone, and that there is no named successor trustee.

The Court authorizes Ms. Dolch to have a subpoena issued to obtain the will located in Ireland.

The matter is continued to February 10, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Musto. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-07-2025 Continued To Dec-09-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Wrobel for petitioner Stephen Courtney (via Zoom).
Ryan Meckfessel for special administrator Debra Dolch, who was also present.

The matter was called for hearing on 1) Amended Petition for Probate of Will and Letters of Administration with Will Annexed; 2) Order to Appear/Order to Show Cause.

The Court noted attorney Mark Rennie's failure to appear and observed that he had previously failed to attend multiple hearings in the case. As a result, the court imposed sanctions in the amount of $250 for failure to appear or respond to the Order to Show Cause.

Discussion was held regarding the status of the related probate proceedings in Ireland. Mr. Wrobel explained that the probate of the will in Ireland had not yet been initiated by the executor's attorney, preventing the issuance of a certified copy of the will. Counsel for the adult children intended to proceed with filings in the Irish court due to the lack of action by the executor's attorney. The Court advised that should Mr. Wrobel wish to have Ms. Dolch appointed as administrator without admitting the will, an amended petition would need to be filed requesting this relief.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to December 9, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow time for to coordinate a plan for moving the matter forward and addressing the related proceedings in Ireland.

Judge: Cindee Mayfield; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Order To Appear [Re: Debra Dolch]
Off Calendar Aug-19-2025 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-19-2025 Continued To Oct-07-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ryan Meckfessel for special administrator Debra Dolch, who was also present.
Thomas Wrobel for petitioner and heir Stephen Courtney.

The Court called the matter on for: 1) an Amended Petition for Probate of Will and Letters of Administration with Will Annexed; 2) Order to Show Cause to Mark Rennie for Failure to Appear; and 3) Order to Appear issued to Debra Dolch.

The Court noted that Ms. Dolch had appeared as ordered, but Mr. Rennie had again failed to appear. The Court imposed sanctions in the amount of $250 against Mr. Rennie for failing to appear and indicated that an additional Order to Show Cause shall be issued as to why additional sanctions should not be imposed for his failure to appear. Hearing on the Order to Show Cause shall be set for October 7, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

The Order to Appear issued to Debra Dolch was taken off calendar.

Mr. Wrobel confirmed that the certified copy of the decedent's will from Ireland had not yet been obtained. He explained that the decedent's surviving spouse, Lola Courtney, had retained Irish counsel to probate the will in Ireland, but the proceedings were delayed due to a pending claim filed by the decedent's children, which had stalled the probate process.

Mr. Meckfessel noted that Ms. Dolch continued to face difficulties acting under letters of special administration, as financial institutions were less responsive than they would be to a fully appointed administrator. He argued that appointment as general administrator without the will annexed would allow her to properly marshal the estate's assets.

The Court inquired about the decedent's business operations and was advised that the grocery store remained profitable.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to October 7, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. An Order to Appear shall be issued to objector/named executor Lola Courtney to appear via Zoom on that date.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-20-2025 Continued To Aug-19-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Thomas Wrobel appeared via zoom for Stephen Courtney (petitioner).

Mr. Wrobel stated that the primary proceedings in Ireland are seeking another executor, that he obtained a Barristers certified copy of the will, and that he will advise Ms. Dolch to seek her own counsel.

An Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause will be issued to Mark Rennie (attorney) to appear on August 19, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to show cause as to why sanctions in the amount of $250.00 should not be imposed against him for failure to appear. An Order to Appear will be issued to Debra Dolch (special administrator) to appear on August 19, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Amended Petition for Probate of Will and Letters of Administration with Will Annexed is also continued to that date.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Thompson. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Mar-04-2025 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-04-2025 Continued To May-20-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Wrobel for Stephen Courtney, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Mark Rennie for Lola Courtney, Objector.

Mr. Wrobel reported that a full-day mediation session was held, and information was exchanged, but no final resolution was reached. The parties reached an interim agreement and intend to continue mediation after further information is exchanged. The court-certified copy of the will remains unavailable, as counsel in Ireland has not responded. Counsel indicated that if probate is not initiated in Ireland within 30 days, Lola Courtney will seek new representation there.

The Court inquired whether the grocery store owned by the estate is still operational. Mr. Rennie confirmed that it is, and business has stabilized post-COVID.

Counsel discussed potential competing probate petitions but stated they are working toward a mediated resolution that may involve a neutral fiduciary. The Court raised the issue of appointing a special administrator with full IAEA authority to make progress. Counsel agreed this may be necessary but wished to confer further.

The court continued the matter to May 20, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, for further updates. The hearing on status of mediation and the order to appear were ordered off calendar.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Mar-04-2025 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Feb-04-2025 Continued To Mar-04-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas S. Wrobel for Stephen Courtney, Petitioner.

Mr. Wrobel reported that despite multiple attempts, mediation has not yet been scheduled.

Discussion was held regarding the status of probate proceedings in Ireland. Mr. Wrobel stated that the will has not been admitted in the Irish court, and there has been no progress in obtaining a certified copy. No direct has been made with the solicitor representing Lola Courtney in Ireland. The court directed Mr. Wrobel to contact the Irish solicitor to determine the cause of the delay in admitting the will.

The court continued the matter to March 4, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The court will issue an Order to Appear to Mr. Mark Rennie, requiring his attendance at the hearing.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-25-2024 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-25-2024 Continued To Oct-08-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Wrobel appeared by zoom for petitioner.
Mark Rennie for Lola Courtney.

The court stated that it waives sanctions in light of Mr. Rennie's response. Mr. Wrobel stated that one hold up of the petition in Ireland is an issue of obtaining a certified death certificate, and that it may take up to six months before one could be obtained. Mr. Rennie stated that a certified death certificate was mailed to the Irish lawyer about a week ago, that he asked the Irish lawyer for a copy of the will, and it took him six months to fax a copy, that he will stay on him and that he is hopeful to be filing a competing petition. Mr. Wrobel suggested a 90-day continuance and contacting the court in about 60-days if there is no progress on receiving a certified will. Counsel also indicated that they are communicating and spoke of how the store is being managed. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than September 24, 2024. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by October 1, 2024. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to and the Parties are to return on October 8, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The order to show cause is off calendar.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Pliego. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-15-2024 Continued To Jun-25-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Wrobel appeared by zoom for petitioner.

The court noted that Attorney Mark Rennie was not present. Mr. Wrobel stated that he had not heard from Mr. Rennie, that there has been no progress with getting the original will from Ireland, and that the will is in the possession of the spouse (Lola). Mr. Wrobel also stated that he was suprised to see that no competing petition has been filed and that the bulk of the assets are in Ireland. The court stated that it has seen a copy of the will, but it is more comfortable with getting an authenticated copy of the will. Mr. Wroble stated that based on the lack of cooperation, it would be appropriate to treat this as an unavailable will. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to June 25, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Wrobel is to reach out to Mr. Rennie regarding the issue of obtaining the will.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Attorney Mark E. Renie to appear on June 25, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why he failed to appear. An order to show cause re sanctions under CCP 128 is also being issued for the same date for Mr. Rennie to appear and show cause as to why sanctions in the amount of $250.00 should not be imposed against him for failure to appear under.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Murphy. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-03-2024 Continued To May-15-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Wrobel appeared by zoom for Stephen Courtney.
Mark Rennie for Lola Courtney (objector).

Mr. Wrobel stated that Stephen obtained the capacity declaration while he was pursuing a conservatorship of Lola. Discussion was held regarding the Courtney Produce store generating income, the issue of whether there are other assets that are not currently known by Stephen, Stephen having been estranged from Patrick, the children's $3,000,000.00 creditors' claim, concern about Lola getting evicted, the decedent's expressed wishes to Mr. Rennie, Mr. Rennie's promise to the decedent to take care of Lola, the possibility of having Melanie Emmons file a petition for Lola, a copy of the Irish will being in the possession of the Attorney in Ireland, Mr. Wrobel position that he may not be able to get a certified copy of the will because he believes Lola's attorney had the original will, etc. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to May 15, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of a competing petition. The court stated that a certified copy of the will needs to be filed.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Nguyen. Not reported.
